Cinderbloom uses the following formula to calculate the damage for its critical strike: {\rm increased\ damage}={\rm base\ damage}+(({\rm base\ damage}\times 0.2)\times ({\rm product\ of\ all\ critical\ damage\ modifiers})).
For example, if an ability deals 100 damage by default, then it will critically strike for 120 damage: {\rm 120}={\rm 100}+({\rm 100}\times 0.2).

If the user has a critical damage modifier such as Infinity Edge, the damage amplifier is increased to 26%. The ability will thus critically strike for 126 damage: {\rm 126}={\rm 100}+(({\rm 100}\times 0.2)\times (1+0.3)).

If the target has critical strike damage reduction such as Randuin's Omen Resilience, which counts as a critical damage modifier for Cinderbloom, the damage amplifier is reduced to 14%. The ability will thus critically strike for 114 damage: {\rm 114}={\rm 100}+(({\rm 100}\times 0.2)\times (1+(-0.3))).

If the user has Infinity Edge and the target has Randuin's Omen, the damage amplifier is reduced to 18.2%, which is only slightly lower than the base amplifier of 20% due to the multiplicative nature of the modifiers. The ability will thus critically strike for 118.2 damage: 118.2=100+100\times 0.2\times (1+0.3)\times(1-0.3).
If the damage dealt already critically strikes, Cinderbloom will stack multiplicatively with the critical strike damage multiplier.

For example, if a damage instance critically strikes for 200% damage, Cinderbloom will stack with that multiplier multiplicatively for a total critical strike of 240% damage: {\rm 2.4}={\rm 2}\times 1.2.
